slides.heading: The Basic Order
slides.content: In English, we say 'I eat apples.' In Japanese, the verb goes at the end: 'I apples eat.'

slides.heading: The Topic Particle (wa)
slides.content: Use 'は' (wa) after the person or thing you are talking about. 'Watashi wa...' means 'As for me...'

slides.heading: Desu (To be)
slides.content: To say 'It is X', we use 'desu'. For example: 'Neko desu' (It is a cat).

slides.heading: Making Questions
slides.content: Simply add 'ka' (か) to the end of any sentence to turn it into a question. No question mark needed!

slides.heading: Politeness
slides.content: Using 'desu' and 'masu' makes you sound polite and respectful to everyone you meet.
